title: Thoughts and Things 3/24/22 (logged 2023)
html:
TL;DR

  • Mid-term-week journal — Yahir blowing past an Econ midterm to vent about ChatGPT and the AI inflection point in real time
  • Cites Life 3.0 from his freshman-year reading list as the moment he "called out" needing to engage with AI — receipts in hand
  • Maps the era-cohorts of tech founders (PC 80s: Gates/Jobs/Ellison; Dotcom 90s: Musk/Cuban/Page; Social 00s–10s: Zuck/Dorsey/Chamath) and asks where his generation's name shows up
  • Validation thread: cites Mari (cross-country pickleball PAC) — "validation comes from within" — and references Chamath's framing on family-validation being diluted
  • Steve Jobs reference: "everything we see around us was made by people no smarter than you or I" — used as the talisman against impostor syndrome
  • Ends shaken-but-anchored: "I can achieve anything the person right next to me can — shoutout Juice WRLD"

Notes

A dense mid-2023 (filename says 3/24/22 but doc was logged 2023 — the ChatGPT context confirms 2023 timing) AI-existential journal entry. Yahir is in college "fucking around" while ChatGPT reshapes the labor landscape, and the doc oscillates between two states:

  1. Nihilistic: jobs are about to be choked out, people who can't reposition will be wiped out, "the singularity is coming and there isn't much we can do"
  2. Optimistic / niche-finding: build a "safe haven pocket" where he leverages the wipeout rather than getting wiped out — but immediately catches himself: that sounds like a "semi-successful Etsy store" and isn't the legacy he wants

The doc is also an inflection point on the validation thread that runs through 2022–2023. He notes (1) family validation is dulled because it's unconditional, (2) Investment Group external validation didn't satisfy, (3) Mari said "validation comes from within" and he believes her, (4) Sahil Bloom's "hours-with-people" graph (sent to Jipp) keeps surfacing.

The era-cohort lineup — Gates/Jobs in the PC 80s, Musk/Cuban in the dotcom 90s, Zuck/Chamath in the social era — frames an unspoken question: "who from my generation gets named?" The Steve Jobs "people no smarter than you or I" line is the antibody.

Worth promoting Mari to a stub if she recurs.

Connects to: 2023 01 30 eli internship (the crypto thesis getting re-asked), founder loneliness 2022 2026.
